_meta.note: 3-8 English Wikipedia article titles per channel, four-category logic (geography, institutions, doctrine, chokepoints): articles a reader would actually visit during a crisis. Structural pages only -- places and institutions, never event pages, and TWO hard requirements enforced by tests/CI and audit: (1) no title may contain a banned event name; (2) every title must have meaningful pre-event attention independent of any validation episode ('Galwan River' failed both -- near-zero pageviews before the June 2020 clash that is itself a validation target -- and was removed 2026-07-24; 'Siachen Glacier' or 'Aksai Chin' are the passing pattern). Every title verified against the pageviews API on the filled_on date; canonical titles matter (data lives under 'CAATSA' and 'Houthi movement', not their redirects).

us_trade: Trade policy of the United States
shipping: Piracy off the coast of Somalia
